query_normalized: the influence of algorithmic recommendations on exploration vs. exploitation in online learning psychology
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by measuring the same core mechanism (balancing exploration vs. exploitation) within a social decision-making context, even though it uses multi-agent reinforcement learning agents rather than human subjects directly. It also aligns with criterion (f) as it belongs to the canonical literature on the exploration-exploitation trade-off in social dilemmas, providing foundational evidence for how this balance influences collective outcomes in online learning enviro
